overview: The June 13-14 window brought a mix of verifier correctness fixes and new networking capabilities. Kuniyuki Iwashima posted a v3 of the RX/TX hardware timestamp proxy series, introducing a new kfunc and the bpf_skb_set_hwtstamp() helper so TC programs can both read and write hardware timestamps on skbs. Multiple verifier patches landed from Nuoqi Gui and Woojin Ji tightening pointer-type preservation on scalar add, zero-spill handling for variable-offset stack reads, and gotox target bounds enforcement, while Alexei Starovoitov doubled the maximum BPF subprogram call chain depth from 8 to 16 frames.

patches.title: bpf: Add bpf_skb_set_hwtstamp().
patches.author: Kuniyuki Iwashima
patches.summary: Part of the v3 'Support RX/TX HW timestamp proxy' series, this patch introduces the bpf_skb_set_hwtstamp() helper that lets BPF TC programs write hardware timestamps onto socket buffers. Combined with the companion kfunc for TX timestamp proxying, the series enables a virtual interface to act as a transparent hardware-timestamp relay between a container's network stack and the underlying physical NIC. This is useful for precision-time applications running in VMs or containers that need accurate hardware timestamping without kernel changes. A self-test is included to validate the proxy behaviour end-to-end.

patches.title: bpf: Add kfunc to proxy TX HW Timestamp.
patches.author: Kuniyuki Iwashima
patches.summary: This patch (also in the v3 hwtstamp proxy series) adds a kfunc that allows a BPF TC program to forward a TX hardware timestamp from a virtual interface to the socket that originally sent the packet. Without this, TX timestamps generated by the physical NIC are lost when the packet path passes through a virtual device layer. The kfunc gives BPF programs a way to bridge that gap, completing the RX/TX timestamp proxy capability introduced in this series.

patches.title: bpf: Support BPF_F_INGRESS with bpf_redirect_peer
patches.author: Jordan Rife
patches.summary: This patch extends bpf_redirect_peer() to accept the BPF_F_INGRESS flag, enabling TC BPF programs to inject a packet directly into the ingress path of a veth peer without a network-namespace traversal. Previously BPF_F_INGRESS was only supported by bpf_redirect(), making bpf_redirect_peer() ingress redirection impossible from TC programs. The change simplifies container networking forwarding paths and removes the need for an intermediate qdisc hop in certain redirect topologies. A companion selftest covers the new flag combination across multiple redirect scenarios.

patches.title: bpf: Raise maximum call chain depth to 16 frames
patches.author: Alexei Starovoitov
patches.summary: This single patch doubles the BPF verifier's maximum subprogram call chain depth from 8 to 16 frames. The original 8-frame cap was a conservative early choice that is now too restrictive for the more complex multi-layer BPF programs that the ecosystem has developed over time. The verifier's per-frame call stack tracking is updated accordingly. The change is low-risk because the verifier already handles unbounded complexity through instruction-count limits and other bounds checks.

patches.title: bpf: Preserve untrusted memory pointer type on scalar add
patches.author: Nuoqi Gui
patches.summary: This verifier fix ensures that adding a scalar to an untrusted memory pointer does not silently promote the result to a more-trusted pointer type. Before this change, certain scalar-add code paths would lose the 'untrusted' annotation on the resulting register, potentially allowing subsequent memory accesses to bypass verifier safety checks. The fix tightens type propagation for scalar arithmetic involving untrusted pointers and closes a potential class of verifier soundness issues. A new selftest exercises the fixed path to prevent regressions.

patches.title: bpf: Preserve scalar zero spills for var-offset stack reads
patches.author: Woojin Ji
patches.summary: This verifier improvement fixes a precision loss when BPF stack slots that hold spilled scalar zero values are later read back via a variable (non-constant) offset. The verifier was discarding zero-spill information during var-offset stack slot analysis, causing either unnecessary rejections of valid programs or inaccurate register range tracking downstream. The patch ensures zero spills are preserved through variable-offset reads, improving the verifier's ability to reason about stack state without introducing false positives. A selftest adds coverage for the previously broken scenario.

patches.title: bpf: Enforce gotox targets against subprog bounds
patches.author: Nuoqi Gui
patches.summary: This verifier security fix adds explicit boundary validation for gotox (extended goto) instruction targets, ensuring they cannot jump outside the bounds of the current subprogram. Without this check a program could craft a gotox that lands in the middle of a different subprogram, bypassing the per-subprogram verification invariants the verifier relies on for safety guarantees. The fix adds a subprogram-range check for all gotox instructions during the verification pass and includes a selftest verifying that out-of-bounds targets are properly rejected.
